rule_text: The 50/50 overhang relies on position:absolute, which Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores (badge drops to top-left in normal flow, box not inset). For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, pre-composite the white badge (with shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG placed via the same absolute positioning, or bake the badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping text live. When not feasible, ship the live version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge); every other rule (Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.
intent: Provide a brand-safe fallback for Outlook's lack of absolute positioning.
summary: Use pre-composited transparent PNG for 50/50 absolute-positioned badge to avoid Outlook desktop/Word engine issues; else accept badge flush inside left edge with other styles unchanged.
